NKJV.1: When you sit down to eat with a ruler, Consider carefully what is before you;
NKJV.2: And put a knife to your throat If you are a man given to appetite.
NKJV.3: Do not desire his delicacies, For they are deceptive food.
NKJV.4: Do not overwork to be rich;  Because of your own understanding, cease!
NKJV.5: Will you set your eyes on that which is not? For riches certainly make themselves wings; They fly away like an eagle toward heaven.
NKJV.6: Do not eat the bread of a miser, Nor desire his delicacies;
NKJV.7: For as he thinks in his heart, so is he. “Eat and drink!” he says to you, But his heart is not with you.
NKJV.8: The morsel you have eaten, you will vomit up, And waste your pleasant words.
NKJV.9: Do not speak in the hearing of a fool, For he will despise the wisdom of your words.
NKJV.10: Do not remove the ancient landmark, Nor enter the fields of the fatherless;
NKJV.11: For their Redeemer is mighty; He will plead their cause against you.
NKJV.12: Apply your heart to instruction, And your ears to words of knowledge.
NKJV.13: Do not withhold correction from a child, For if you beat him with a rod, he will not die.
NKJV.14: You shall beat him with a rod, And deliver his soul from hell.
NKJV.15: My son, if your heart is wise, My heart will rejoice—indeed, I myself;
NKJV.16: Yes, my inmost being will rejoice When your lips speak right things.
NKJV.17: Do not let your heart envy sinners, But be zealous for the fear of the LORD all the day;
NKJV.18: For surely there is a hereafter, And your hope will not be cut off.
NKJV.19: Hear, my son, and be wise; And guide your heart in the way.
NKJV.20: Do not mix with winebibbers, Or with gluttonous eaters of meat;
NKJV.21: For the drunkard and the glutton will come to poverty, And drowsiness will clothe a man with rags.
NKJV.22: Listen to your father who begot you, And do not despise your mother when she is old.
NKJV.23: Buy the truth, and do not sell it, Also wisdom and instruction and understanding.
NKJV.24: The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice, And he who begets a wise child will delight in him.
NKJV.25: Let your father and your mother be glad, And let her who bore you rejoice.
NKJV.26: My son, give me your heart, And let your eyes observe my ways.
NKJV.27: For a harlot is a deep pit, And a seductress is a narrow well.
NKJV.28: She also lies in wait as for a victim, And increases the unfaithful among men.
NKJV.29: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has contentions? Who has complaints? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes?
NKJV.30: Those who linger long at the wine, Those who go in search of mixed wine.
NKJV.31: Do not look on the wine when it is red, When it sparkles in the cup, When it swirls around smoothly;
NKJV.32: At the last it bites like a serpent, And stings like a viper.
NKJV.33: Your eyes will see strange things, And your heart will utter perverse things.
NKJV.34: Yes, you will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea, Or like one who lies at the top of the mast, saying:
NKJV.35: “They have struck me, but I was not hurt; They have beaten me, but I did not feel it. When shall I awake, that I may seek another drink?”

ESV.1: When you sit down to eat with a ruler, observe carefully what  is before you,
ESV.2: and put a knife to your throat if you are given to appetite.
ESV.3: Do not desire his delicacies, for they are deceptive food.
ESV.4: Do not toil to acquire wealth; be discerning enough to desist.
ESV.5: When your eyes light on it, it is gone, for suddenly it sprouts wings, flying like an eagle toward heaven.
ESV.6: Do not eat the bread of a man who is stingy;  do not desire his delicacies,
ESV.7: for he is like one who is inwardly calculating.  “Eat and drink!” he says to you, but his heart is not with you.
ESV.8: You will vomit up the morsels that you have eaten, and waste your pleasant words.
ESV.9: Do not speak in the hearing of a fool, for he will despise the good sense of your words.
ESV.10: Do not move an ancient landmark or enter the fields of the fatherless,
ESV.11: for their Redeemer is strong; he will plead their cause against you.
ESV.12: Apply your heart to instruction and your ear to words of knowledge.
ESV.13: Do not withhold discipline from a child; if you strike him with a rod, he will not die.
ESV.14: If you strike him with the rod, you will save his soul from Sheol.
ESV.15: My son, if your heart is wise, my heart too will be glad.
ESV.16: My inmost being  will exult when your lips speak what is right.
ESV.17: Let not your heart envy sinners, but continue in the fear of the LORD all the day.
ESV.18: Surely there is a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
ESV.19: Hear, my son, and be wise, and direct your heart in the way.
ESV.20: Be not among drunkards  or among gluttonous eaters of meat,
ESV.21: for the drunkard and the glutton will come to poverty, and slumber will clothe them with rags.
ESV.22: Listen to your father who gave you life, and do not despise your mother when she is old.
ESV.23: Buy truth, and do not sell it; buy wisdom, instruction, and understanding.
ESV.24: The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice; he who fathers a wise son will be glad in him.
ESV.25: Let your father and mother be glad; let her who bore you rejoice.
ESV.26: My son, give me your heart, and let your eyes observe  my ways.
ESV.27: For a prostitute is a deep pit; an adulteress  is a narrow well.
ESV.28: She lies in wait like a robber and increases the traitors among mankind.
ESV.29: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has strife? Who has complaining? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes?
ESV.30: Those who tarry long over wine; those who go to try mixed wine.
ESV.31: Do not look at wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup and goes down smoothly.
ESV.32: In the end it bites like a serpent and stings like an adder.
ESV.33: Your eyes will see strange things, and your heart utter perverse things.
ESV.34: You will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea, like one who lies on the top of a mast.
ESV.35: “They struck me,” you will say,  “but I was not hurt; they beat me, but I did not feel it. When shall I awake? I must have another drink.”

NASB.1: When you sit down to dine with a ruler, Consider carefully what is before you,
NASB.2: And put a knife to your throat If you are a man of [great] appetite.
NASB.3: Do not desire his delicacies, For it is deceptive food.
NASB.4: Do not weary yourself to gain wealth, Cease from your consideration [of it].
NASB.5: When you set your eyes on it, it is gone. For [wealth] certainly makes itself wings Like an eagle that flies [toward] the heavens.
NASB.6: Do not eat the bread of a selfish man, Or desire his delicacies;
NASB.7: For as he thinks within himself, so he is. He says to you, 'Eat and drink!' But his heart is not with you.
NASB.8: You will vomit up the morsel you have eaten, And waste your compliments.
NASB.9: Do not speak in the hearing of a fool, For he will despise the wisdom of your words.
NASB.10: Do not move the ancient boundary Or go into the fields of the fatherless,
NASB.11: For their Redeemer is strong; He will plead their case against you.
NASB.12: Apply your heart to discipline And your ears to words of knowledge.
NASB.13: Do not hold back discipline from the child, Although you strike him with the rod, he will not die.
NASB.14: You shall strike him with the rod And rescue his soul from Sheol.
NASB.15: My son, if your heart is wise, My own heart also will be glad;
NASB.16: And my inmost being will rejoice When your lips speak what is right.
NASB.17: Do not let your heart envy sinners, But [live] in the fear of the LORD always.
NASB.18: Surely there is a future, And your hope will not be cut off.
NASB.19: Listen, my son, and be wise, And direct your heart in the way.
NASB.20: Do not be with heavy drinkers of wine, [Or] with gluttonous eaters of meat;
NASB.21: For the heavy drinker and the glutton will come to poverty, And drowsiness will clothe [one] with rags.
NASB.22: Listen to your father who begot you, And do not despise your mother when she is old.
NASB.23: Buy truth, and do not sell [it], [Get] wisdom and instruction and understanding.
NASB.24: The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice, And he who sires a wise son will be glad in him.
NASB.25: Let your father and your mother be glad, And let her rejoice who gave birth to you.
NASB.26: Give me your heart, my son, And let your eyes delight in my ways.
NASB.27: For a harlot is a deep pit And an adulterous woman is a narrow well.
NASB.28: Surely she lurks as a robber, And increases the faithless among men.
NASB.29: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has contentions? Who has complaining? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes?
NASB.30: Those who linger long over wine, Those who go to taste mixed wine.
NASB.31: Do not look on the wine when it is red, When it sparkles in the cup, When it goes down smoothly;
NASB.32: At the last it bites like a serpent And stings like a viper.
NASB.33: Your eyes will see strange things And your mind will utter perverse things.
NASB.34: And you will be like one who lies down in the middle of the sea, Or like one who lies down on the top of a mast.
NASB.35: 'They struck me, [but] I did not become ill; They beat me, [but] I did not know [it]. When shall I awake? I will seek another drink.'

NLT.1:
While dining with a ruler,
pay attention to what is put before you.
NLT.2:
If you are a big eater,
put a knife to your throat;
NLT.3:
don’t desire all the delicacies,
for he might be trying to trick you.
NLT.4:
Don’t wear yourself out trying to get rich.
Be wise enough to know when to quit.
NLT.5:
In the blink of an eye wealth disappears,
for it will sprout wings
and fly away like an eagle.
NLT.6:
Don’t eat with people who are stingy;
don’t desire their delicacies.
NLT.7:
They are always thinking about how much it costs.
“Eat and drink,” they say, but they don’t mean it.
NLT.8:
You will throw up what little you’ve eaten,
and your compliments will be wasted.
NLT.9:
Don’t waste your breath on fools,
for they will despise the wisest advice.
NLT.10:
Don’t cheat your neighbor by moving the ancient boundary markers;
don’t take the land of defenseless orphans.
NLT.11:
For their Redeemer is strong;
he himself will bring their charges against you.
NLT.12:
Commit yourself to instruction;
listen carefully to words of knowledge.
NLT.13:
Don’t fail to discipline your children.
The rod of punishment won’t kill them.
NLT.14:
Physical discipline
may well save them from death.
NLT.15:
My child, if your heart is wise,
my own heart will rejoice!
NLT.16:
Everything in me will celebrate
when you speak what is right.
NLT.17:
Don’t envy sinners,
but always continue to fear the LORD.
NLT.18:
You will be rewarded for this;
your hope will not be disappointed.
NLT.19:
My child, listen and be wise:
Keep your heart on the right course.
NLT.20:
Do not carouse with drunkards
or feast with gluttons,
NLT.21:
for they are on their way to poverty,
and too much sleep clothes them in rags.
NLT.22:
Listen to your father, who gave you life,
and don’t despise your mother when she is old.
NLT.23:
Get the truth and never sell it;
also get wisdom, discipline, and good judgment.
NLT.24:
The father of godly children has cause for joy.
What a pleasure to have children who are wise.
NLT.25:
So give your father and mother joy!
May she who gave you birth be happy.
NLT.26:
O my son, give me your heart.
May your eyes take delight in following my ways.
NLT.27:
A prostitute is a dangerous trap;
a promiscuous woman is as dangerous as falling into a narrow well.
NLT.28:
She hides and waits like a robber,
eager to make more men unfaithful.
NLT.29:
Who has anguish? Who has sorrow?
Who is always fighting? Who is always complaining?
Who has unnecessary bruises? Who has bloodshot eyes?
NLT.30:
It is the one who spends long hours in the taverns,
trying out new drinks.
NLT.31:
Don’t gaze at the wine, seeing how red it is,
how it sparkles in the cup, how smoothly it goes down.
NLT.32:
For in the end it bites like a poisonous snake;
it stings like a viper.
NLT.33:
You will see hallucinations,
and you will say crazy things.
NLT.34:
You will stagger like a sailor tossed at sea,
clinging to a swaying mast.
NLT.35:
And you will say, “They hit me, but I didn’t feel it.
I didn’t even know it when they beat me up.
When will I wake up
so I can look for another drink?”

WEB.1: When you sit to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what is before you;
WEB.2: put a knife to your throat, if you are a man given to appetite.
WEB.3: Don’t be desirous of his dainties, since they are deceitful food.
WEB.4: Don’t weary yourself to be rich. In your wisdom, show restraint.
WEB.5: Why do you set your eyes on that which is not? For it certainly sprouts wings like an eagle and flies in the sky.
WEB.6: Don’t eat the food of him who has a stingy eye, and don’t crave his delicacies:
WEB.7: for as he thinks about the cost, so he is. “Eat and drink!” he says to you, but his heart is not with you.
WEB.8: The morsel which you have eaten you shall vomit up, and lose your good words.
WEB.9: Don’t speak in the ears of a fool, for he will despise the wisdom of your words.
WEB.10: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone. Don’t encroach on the fields of the fatherless:
WEB.11: for their Defender is strong. He will plead their case against you.
WEB.12: Apply your heart to instruction, and your ears to the words of knowledge.
WEB.13: Don’t withhold correction from a child. If you punish him with the rod, he will not die.
WEB.14: Punish him with the rod, and save his soul from Sheol.
WEB.15: My son, if your heart is wise, then my heart will be glad, even mine:
WEB.16: yes, my heart will rejoice, when your lips speak what is right.
WEB.17: Don’t let your heart envy sinners; but rather fear Yahweh all day long.
WEB.18: Indeed surely there is a future hope, and your hope will not be cut off.
WEB.19: Listen, my son, and be wise, and keep your heart on the right path!
WEB.20: Don’t be among ones drinking too much wine, or those who gorge themselves on meat:
WEB.21: for the drunkard and the glutton shall become poor; and drowsiness clothes them in rags.
WEB.22: Listen to your father who gave you life, and don’t despise your mother when she is old.
WEB.23: Buy the truth, and don’t sell it. Get wisdom, discipline, and understanding.
WEB.24: The father of the righteous has great joy. Whoever fathers a wise child delights in him.
WEB.25: Let your father and your mother be glad! Let her who bore you rejoice!
WEB.26: My son, give me your heart; and let your eyes keep in my ways.
WEB.27: For a prostitute is a deep pit; and a wayward wife is a narrow well.
WEB.28: Yes, she lies in wait like a robber, and increases the unfaithful among men.
WEB.29: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has strife? Who has complaints? Who has needless bruises? Who has bloodshot eyes?
WEB.30: Those who stay long at the wine; those who go to seek out mixed wine.
WEB.31: Don’t look at the wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup, when it goes down smoothly.
WEB.32: In the end, it bites like a snake, and poisons like a viper.
WEB.33: Your eyes will see strange things, and your mind will imagine confusing things.
WEB.34: Yes, you will be as he who lies down in the middle of the sea, or as he who lies on top of the rigging:
WEB.35: “They hit me, and I was not hurt! They beat me, and I don’t feel it! When will I wake up? I can do it again. I can find another.”

Don’t let your heart envy sinners; ⓐ
instead, always fear the LORD. ⓑ
CSB17.18:
For then you will have a future,
and your hope will not be dashed. ⓒ
CSB17.19:
Listen, my son, and be wise;
keep your mind on the right course.
CSB17.20:
Don’t associate with those who drink too much wine ⓓ
or with those who gorge themselves on meat. ⓔ
CSB17.21:
For the drunkard and the glutton will become poor,
and grogginess will clothe them in rags. ⓕ
CSB17.22:
Listen to your father who gave you life,
and don’t despise your mother when [6] she is old. ⓖ
CSB17.23:
Buy — and do not sell — truth,
wisdom, instruction, and understanding. ⓗ
CSB17.24:
The father of a righteous son will rejoice greatly,
and one who fathers a wise son will delight in him. ⓘ
CSB17.25:
Let your father and mother have joy, ⓙ
and let her who gave birth to you rejoice.
CSB17.26:
My son, give me your heart,
and let your eyes observe my ways.
CSB17.27:
For a prostitute is a deep pit, ⓚ
and a wayward woman is a narrow well;
CSB17.28:
indeed, she sets an ambush like a robber ⓛ
and increases the number of unfaithful people.
CSB17.29:
Who has woe? Who has sorrow?
Who has conflicts? Who has complaints?
Who has wounds for no reason? ⓜ
Who has red eyes?
CSB17.30:
Those who linger over wine;
those who go looking for mixed wine. ⓝ
CSB17.31:
Don’t gaze at wine because it is red, ⓐ
because it gleams in the cup
and goes down smoothly. ⓑ
CSB17.32:
In the end it bites like a snake
and stings like a viper.
CSB17.33:
Your eyes will see strange things,
and you will say absurd things. [7] ⓒ
CSB17.34:
You’ll be like someone sleeping out at sea
or lying down on the top of a ship’s mast.
CSB17.35:
“They struck me, but [8] I feel no pain!
They beat me, but I didn’t know it! ⓓ
When will I wake up?
I’ll look for another drink.” ⓔ

AMP.1:
When you sit down to dine with a ruler,
Consider carefully what is [set] before you;
AMP.2:
For you will put a knife to your throat
If you are a man of great appetite.
AMP.3:
Do not desire his delicacies,
For it is deceptive food [offered to you with questionable motives].
AMP.4:
Do not weary yourself [with the overwhelming desire] to gain wealth;
Cease from your own understanding of it.
AMP.5:
When you set your eyes on wealth, it is [suddenly] gone.
For wealth certainly makes itself wings
Like an eagle that flies to the heavens.
AMP.6:
Do not eat the bread of a selfish man,
Or desire his delicacies;
AMP.7:
For as he thinks in his heart, so is he [in behavior—one who manipulates].
He says to you, "Eat and drink,"
Yet his heart is not with you [but it is begrudging the cost].
AMP.8:
The morsel which you have eaten you will vomit up,
And you will waste your compliments.
AMP.9:
Do not speak in the ears of a fool,
For he will despise the [godly] wisdom of your words.
AMP.10:
Do not move the ancient landmark [at the boundary of the property]
And do not go into the fields of the fatherless [to take what is theirs],
AMP.11:
For their Redeemer is strong and mighty;
He will plead their case against you.
AMP.12:
Apply your heart to discipline
And your ears to words of knowledge.
AMP.13:
Do not withhold discipline from the child;
If you swat him with a reed-like rod [applied with godly wisdom], he will not die.
AMP.14:
You shall swat him with the reed-like rod
And rescue his life from Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead).
AMP.15:
My son, if your heart is wise,
My heart will also be glad;
AMP.16:
Yes, my heart will rejoice
When your lips speak right things.
AMP.17:
Do not let your heart envy sinners [who live godless lives and have no hope of salvation],
But [continue to] live in the [reverent, worshipful] fear of the LORD day by day.
AMP.18:
Surely there is a future [and a reward],
And your hope and expectation will not be cut off.
AMP.19:
Listen, my son, and be wise,
And direct your heart in the way [of the LORD].
AMP.20:
Do not associate with heavy drinkers of wine,
Or with gluttonous eaters of meat,
AMP.21:
For the heavy drinker and the glutton will come to poverty,
And the drowsiness [of overindulgence] will clothe one with rags.
AMP.22:
Listen to your father, who sired you,
And do not despise your mother when she is old.
AMP.23:
Buy truth, and do not sell it;
Get wisdom and instruction and understanding.
AMP.24:
The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice,
And he who sires a wise child will have joy in him.
AMP.25:
Let your father and your mother be glad,
And let her who gave birth to you rejoice [in your wise and godly choices].
AMP.26:
My son, give me your heart
And let your eyes delight in my ways,
AMP.27:
For a prostitute is a deep pit,
And an immoral woman is a narrow well.
AMP.28:
She lurks and lies in wait like a robber [who waits for prey],
And she increases the faithless among men.
AMP.29:
Who has woe? Who has sorrow?
Who has strife? Who has complaining?
Who has wounds without cause?
Whose eyes are red and dim?
AMP.30:
Those who linger long over wine,
Those who go to taste mixed wine.
AMP.31:
Do not look at wine when it is red,
When it sparkles in the glass,
When it goes down smoothly.
AMP.32:
At the last it bites like a serpent
And stings like a viper.
AMP.33:
Your [drunken] eyes will see strange things
And your mind will utter perverse things [untrue things, twisted things].
AMP.34:
And you will be [as unsteady] as one who lies down in the middle of the sea,
And [as vulnerable to disaster] as one who lies down on the top of a ship's mast, saying,
AMP.35:
"They struck me, but I was not hurt!
They beat me, but I did not feel it!
When will I wake up?
I will seek more wine."

MSG.1:
When you go out to dinner with an influential person,
mind your manners:
MSG.2:
Don't gobble your food,
don't talk with your mouth full.
MSG.3:
And don't stuff yourself;
bridle your appetite.
MSG.4:
Don't wear yourself out trying to get rich;
restrain yourself!
MSG.5:
Riches disappear in the blink of an eye;
wealth sprouts wings
and flies off into the wild blue yonder.
MSG.6:
Don't accept a meal from a tightwad;
don't expect anything special.
MSG.7:
He'll be as stingy with you as he is with himself;
he'll say, "Eat! Drink!" but won't mean a word of it.
MSG.8:
His miserly serving will turn your stomach
when you realize the meal's a sham.
MSG.9:
Don't bother talking sense to fools;
they'll only poke fun at your words.
MSG.10:
Don't stealthily move back the boundary lines
or cheat orphans out of their property,
MSG.11:
For they have a powerful Advocate
who will go to bat for them.
MSG.12:
Give yourselves to disciplined instruction;
open your ears to tested knowledge.
MSG.13:
Don't be afraid to correct your young ones;
a spanking won't kill them.
MSG.14:
A good spanking, in fact, might save them
from something worse than death.
MSG.15:
Dear child, if you become wise,
I'll be one happy parent.
MSG.16:
My heart will dance and sing
to the tuneful truth you'll speak.
MSG.17:
Don't for a minute envy careless rebels;
soak yourself in the Fear-of-God—
MSG.18:
That's where your future lies.
Then you won't be left with an armload of nothing.
MSG.19:
Oh listen, dear child—become wise;
point your life in the right direction.
MSG.20:
Don't drink too much wine and get drunk;
don't eat too much food and get fat.
MSG.21:
Drunks and gluttons will end up on skid row,
in a stupor and dressed in rags.
MSG.22:
Listen with respect to the father who raised you,
and when your mother grows old, don't neglect her.
MSG.23:
Buy truth—don't sell it for love or money;
buy wisdom, buy education, buy insight.
MSG.24:
Parents rejoice when their children turn out well;
wise children become proud parents.
MSG.25:
So make your father happy!
Make your mother proud!
MSG.26:
Dear child, I want your full attention;
please do what I show you.
MSG.27:
A whore is a bottomless pit;
a loose woman can get you in deep trouble fast.
MSG.28:
She'll take you for all you've got;
she's worse than a pack of thieves.
MSG.29:
Who are the people who are always crying the blues?
Who do you know who reeks of self-pity?
Who keeps getting beat up for no reason at all?
Whose eyes are bleary and bloodshot?
MSG.30:
It's those who spend the night with a bottle,
for whom drinking is serious business.
MSG.31:
Don't judge wine by its label,
or its bouquet, or its full-bodied flavor.
MSG.32:
Judge it rather by the hangover it leaves you with—
the splitting headache, the queasy stomach.
MSG.33:
Do you really prefer seeing double,
with your speech all slurred,
MSG.34:
Reeling and seasick,
drunk as a sailor?
MSG.35:
"They hit me," you'll say, "but it didn't hurt;
they beat on me, but I didn't feel a thing.
When I'm sober enough to manage it,
bring me another drink!"

NIV.1:
When you sit to dine with a ruler,
note well what is before you,
NIV.2:
and put a knife to your throat
if you are given to gluttony.
NIV.3:
Do not crave his delicacies,
for that food is deceptive.
NIV.4:
Do not wear yourself out to get rich;
have the wisdom to show restraint.
NIV.5:
Cast but a glance at riches, and they are gone,
for they will surely sprout wings
and fly off to the sky like an eagle.
NIV.6:
Do not eat the food of a stingy man,
do not crave his delicacies;
NIV.7:
for he is the kind of man
who is always thinking about the cost.
“Eat and drink,” he says to you,
but his heart is not with you.
NIV.8:
You will vomit up the little you have eaten
and will have wasted your compliments.
NIV.9:
Do not speak to a fool,
for he will scorn the wisdom of your words.
NIV.10:
Do not move an ancient boundary stone
or encroach on the fields of the fatherless,
NIV.11:
for their Defender is strong;
he will take up their case against you.
NIV.12:
Apply your heart to instruction
and your ears to words of knowledge.
NIV.13:
Do not withhold discipline from a child;
if you punish him with the rod, he will not die.
NIV.14:
Punish him with the rod
and save his soul from death.
NIV.15:
My son, if your heart is wise,
then my heart will be glad;
NIV.16:
my inmost being will rejoice
when your lips speak what is right.
NIV.17:
Do not let your heart envy sinners,
but always be zealous for the fear of the Lord.
NIV.18:
There is surely a future hope for you,
and your hope will not be cut off.
NIV.19:
Listen, my son, and be wise,
and keep your heart on the right path.
NIV.20:
Do not join those who drink too much wine
or gorge themselves on meat,
NIV.21:
for drunkards and gluttons become poor,
and drowsiness clothes them in rags.
NIV.22:
Listen to your father, who gave you life,
and do not despise your mother when she is old.
NIV.23:
Buy the truth and do not sell it;
get wisdom, discipline and understanding.
NIV.24:
The father of a righteous man has great joy;
he who has a wise son delights in him.
NIV.25:
May your father and mother be glad;
may she who gave you birth rejoice!
NIV.26:
My son, give me your heart
and let your eyes keep to my ways,
NIV.27:
for a prostitute is a deep pit
and a wayward wife is a narrow well.
NIV.28:
Like a bandit she lies in wait,
and multiplies the unfaithful among men.
NIV.29:
Who has woe? Who has sorrow?
Who has strife? Who has complaints?
Who has needless bruises? Who has bloodshot eyes?
NIV.30:
Those who linger over wine,
who go to sample bowls of mixed wine.
NIV.31:
Do not gaze at wine when it is red,
when it sparkles in the cup,
when it goes down smoothly!
NIV.32:
In the end it bites like a snake
and poisons like a viper.
NIV.33:
Your eyes will see strange sights
and your mind imagine confusing things.
NIV.34:
You will be like one sleeping on the high seas,
lying on top of the rigging.
NIV.35:
“They hit me,” you will say, “but I’m not hurt!
They beat me, but I don’t feel it!
When will I wake up
so I can find another drink?”

NRSVCE.1:
When you sit down to eat with a ruler,
observe carefully what is before you,
NRSVCE.2:
and put a knife to your throat
if you have a big appetite.
NRSVCE.3:
Do not desire the ruler’s delicacies,
for they are deceptive food.
NRSVCE.4:
Do not wear yourself out to get rich;
be wise enough to desist.
NRSVCE.5:
When your eyes light upon it, it is gone;
for suddenly it takes wings to itself,
flying like an eagle toward heaven.
NRSVCE.6:
Do not eat the bread of the stingy;
do not desire their delicacies;
NRSVCE.7:
for like a hair in the throat, so are they.
“Eat and drink!” they say to you;
but they do not mean it.
NRSVCE.8:
You will vomit up the little you have eaten,
and you will waste your pleasant words.
NRSVCE.9:
Do not speak in the hearing of a fool,
who will only despise the wisdom of your words.
NRSVCE.10:
Do not remove an ancient landmark
or encroach on the fields of orphans,
NRSVCE.11:
for their redeemer is strong;
he will plead their cause against you.
NRSVCE.12:
Apply your mind to instruction
and your ear to words of knowledge.
NRSVCE.13:
Do not withhold discipline from your children;
if you beat them with a rod, they will not die.
NRSVCE.14:
If you beat them with the rod,
you will save their lives from Sheol.
NRSVCE.15:
My child, if your heart is wise,
my heart too will be glad.
NRSVCE.16:
My soul will rejoice
when your lips speak what is right.
NRSVCE.17:
Do not let your heart envy sinners,
but always continue in the fear of the LORD.
NRSVCE.18:
Surely there is a future,
and your hope will not be cut off.
NRSVCE.19:
Hear, my child, and be wise,
and direct your mind in the way.
NRSVCE.20:
Do not be among winebibbers,
or among gluttonous eaters of meat;
NRSVCE.21:
for the drunkard and the glutton will come to poverty,
and drowsiness will clothe them with rags.
NRSVCE.22:
Listen to your father who begot you,
and do not despise your mother when she is old.
NRSVCE.23:
Buy truth, and do not sell it;
buy wisdom, instruction, and understanding.
NRSVCE.24:
The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice;
he who begets a wise son will be glad in him.
NRSVCE.25:
Let your father and mother be glad;
let her who bore you rejoice.
NRSVCE.26:
My child, give me your heart,
and let your eyes observe my ways.
NRSVCE.27:
For a prostitute is a deep pit;
an adulteress is a narrow well.
NRSVCE.28:
She lies in wait like a robber
and increases the number of the faithless.
NRSVCE.29:
Who has woe? Who has sorrow?
Who has strife? Who has complaining?
Who has wounds without cause?
Who has redness of eyes?
NRSVCE.30:
Those who linger late over wine,
those who keep trying mixed wines.
NRSVCE.31:
Do not look at wine when it is red,
when it sparkles in the cup
and goes down smoothly.
NRSVCE.32:
At the last it bites like a serpent,
and stings like an adder.
NRSVCE.33:
Your eyes will see strange things,
and your mind utter perverse things.
NRSVCE.34:
You will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea,
like one who lies on the top of a mast.
NRSVCE.35:
“They struck me,” you will say, “but I was not hurt;
they beat me, but I did not feel it.
When shall I awake?
I will seek another drink.”

NET.1: When you sit down to eat with a ruler, consider carefully what is before you,
NET.2: and put a knife to your throat if you possess a large appetite.
NET.3: Do not crave that ruler’s delicacies, for that food is deceptive.
NET.4: Do not wear yourself out to become rich; be wise enough to restrain yourself.
NET.5: When you gaze upon riches, they are gone, for they surely make wings for themselves, and fly off into the sky like an eagle!
NET.6: Do not eat the food of a stingy person, do not crave his delicacies;
NET.7: for he is like someone calculating the cost in his mind. “Eat and drink,” he says to you, but his heart is not with you;
NET.8: you will vomit up the little bit you have eaten, and will have wasted your pleasant words.
NET.9: Do not speak in the ears of a fool, for he will despise the wisdom of your words.
NET.10: Do not move an ancient boundary stone, or take over the fields of the fatherless,
NET.11: for their Protector is strong; he will plead their case against you.
NET.12: Apply your heart to instruction and your ears to the words of knowledge.
NET.13: Do not withhold discipline from a child; even if you strike him with the rod, he will not die.
NET.14: If you strike him with the rod, you will deliver him from death.
NET.15: My child, if your heart is wise, then my heart also will be glad;
NET.16: my soul will rejoice when your lips speak what is right.
NET.17: Do not let your heart envy sinners, but rather be zealous in fearing the LORD all the time.
NET.18: For surely there is a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
NET.19: Listen, my child, and be wise, and guide your heart on the right way.
NET.20: Do not spend time among drunkards, among those who eat too much meat,
NET.21: because drunkards and gluttons become impoverished, and drowsiness clothes them with rags.
NET.22: Listen to your father who begot you, and do not despise your mother when she is old.
NET.23: Acquire truth and do not sell it — wisdom, and discipline, and understanding.
NET.24: The father of a righteous person will rejoice greatly; whoever fathers a wise child will have joy in him.
NET.25: May your father and your mother have joy; may she who bore you rejoice.
NET.26: Give me your heart, my son, and let your eyes observe my ways;
NET.27: for a prostitute is like a deep pit; a harlot is like a narrow well.
NET.28: Indeed, she lies in wait like a robber, and increases the unfaithful among men.
NET.29: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has contentions? Who has complaints? Who has wounds without cause? Who has dullness of the eyes?
NET.30: Those who linger over wine, those who go looking for mixed wine.
NET.31: Do not look on the wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup, when it goes down smoothly.
NET.32: Afterward it bites like a snake, and stings like a viper.
NET.33: Your eyes will see strange things, and your mind will speak perverse things.
NET.34: And you will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea, and like one who lies down on the top of the rigging.
NET.35: You will say, “They have struck me, but I am not harmed! They beat me, but I did not know it! When will I awake? I will look for another drink.”
